Title: Lost but unharmed: Four women found safe after Bukit Tunggul hike
Article:
SEPANG: Four women who became lost while hiking at Bukit Tunggul were found safe and brought out of the area by firefighters today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the
hikers had lost their way
during the climb.
"A search operation was launched after contact was established with the victims, who were aged between 25 and 40.
"All four women were successfully located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area. None of them sustained any injuries," he said when contacted.
Mukhlis said personnel from the Dengkil Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene at 10.50am and immediately began efforts to trace the hikers.
The operation, involving six firefighters led by operations commander Muhamad Sakirin Selamat, was completed at 11.30am.
The hikers were assessed at the scene and did not require further medical treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Produce evidence of alleged Negri Sembilan plot - Asyraf
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has rejected allegations that the party was involved in efforts to destabilise or bring down the Negri Sembilan state government, said its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
Asyraf said that if there were allegations that Umno had been involved in any undemocratic effort to bring down the state government before the end of its term, such allegations must be accompanied by clear evidence and facts.
"Do not throw general accusations that create the perception that Umno has acted disloyally," he told the New Straits Times today.
Asyraf said Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) had provided support to the state government until the expiry of its mandate, and had remained committed to ensuring administrative stability throughout that period.
"Umno has never betrayed the commitments that were agreed upon," he said.
He said once the state assembly's mandate had ended and elections were called, every party had the democratic right to determine its own position, strategy and offer a fresh mandate to the people.
"Competition in elections cannot be equated with betrayal or efforts to topple a government," he said.
He highlighted that Umno continued to respect the spirit of the unity government at the federal level, but said political cooperation did not mean any party had to sacrifice its identity, principles and right to seek a mandate from the people in an election.
Earlier today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim maintained that efforts to destabilise the Negri Sembilan government amounted to a form of betrayal, even as Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi
rejected suggestions
that Umno had acted disloyally.
Anwar said his position was based on what he saw as deliberate attempts to undermine a government that had been functioning in conditions of political and economic stability.
Yesterday, Zahid, who is also Umno president,
rejected claims
that the party had betrayed
its allies in Negri Sembilan, saying there was no question of disloyalty once a government's term had ended.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khind expands rent-to-own push with first physical store launch
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Khind Malaysia has launched its first physical rent-to-own (RTO) store in Semenyih, expanding its consumer financing model beyond digital platforms as it continues to evolve from its roots as a small electrical repair shop.
Founded in 1961 in Sekinchan, Selangor, Khind began as a modest electrical repair business before gradually expanding through product innovation, distribution growth and brand development into a home solutions company.
The company, operated by the Cheng family and listed on Bursa Malaysia as Khind Holdings Bhd, said the milestone reflects decades of transformation driven by continuous innovation in household appliances and customer-focused solutions.
The new outlet marks a key step in Khind's RTO strategy, which allows consumers to access essential home appliances such as washer-dryers, refrigerators and air-conditioning units through flexible monthly payments instead of upfront purchases, aimed at improving affordability for households managing rising living costs.
"We chose Semenyih because it represents the growth of new Malaysian communities, including young families, first-time homeowners, and rapidly growing townships," said Khind Group executive chairman Cheng Ping Keat.
"Our role is to support them by removing the burden of high upfront costs and making essential appliances more accessible and manageable through a monthly plan," he added.
The expansion strengthens the company's RTO presence beyond digital channels, bringing its home appliance ownership solutions closer to local communities while supporting future growth beyond the Klang Valley.
The Semenyih store is designed as a real home-like environment where customers can explore appliances in practical living setups while receiving direct consultation from RTO advisors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid welcomes new political party, urges unity
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has welcomed the formation of a new Malay-based political party, expressing hope that it would strengthen unity rather than divide the community.
He said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new political platform, provided it contributed towards uniting Malays and advancing national interests.
"We welcome any individual or leader, as long as we can unite our thinking and avoid further divisions among ourselves.
"Whatever platform is used, if it ultimately helps unite our actions in support of the national agenda, the Islamic agenda and the interests of our community, then, God willing, we can lend our support.
"We have also come together previously through discourse and discussions under the National Collaboration initiative," he said after attending the Zikir Peringatan Haul Asmaulhusna 2026 programme at Sultan Iskandar Mosque in Bandar Dato' Onn here today.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said differing views should be channelled towards strengthening the national agenda, Islam and the interests of Malays, while continuing to respect the rights of other races and religions in the country.
He added that Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative had successfully contributed to fostering national unity.
Yesterday,
Gerakan Reset Malaysia chairman Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudi
n had announced that the movement would operate under a newly established political party known as Parti Wawasan Negara as part of efforts to strengthen political cooperation with various blocs.
Hamzah had said the party would serve as a platform for unity and work alongside PAS and other like-minded parties to build a broader and more convincing political understanding.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BTS ticket scam: Police track suspected mule bank accounts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in scams involving ticket sales for South Korean K-pop supergroup BTS's concert in December.
Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing, with several bank accounts identified so far.
"We suspect these accounts are being used by syndicates to receive payments from victims.
"It is possible that these bank accounts are mule accounts," he said when contacted today.
The BTS World Tour 'Arirang' in Kuala Lumpur is scheduled for Dec 12-13.
The seven-member South Korean group have previously sold out major venues like London's Wembley Stadium and the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California.
Previously, Hoo said all 28 police reports lodged were under investigation and urged fans to buy tickets only through official channels provided by concert organisers.
Police believe victims were lured by social media advertisements and offers from unauthorised agents claiming to have access to discounted tickets or seats that had already sold out.
The warning comes amid overwhelming demand for BTS concert tickets, which sold out shortly after sales opened, creating opportunities for scammers to target fans seeking alternative sources.
In one reported case, a local fan allegedly lost RM3,000 after dealing with a ticket reseller on social media. The victim made several payments before the seller disappeared, deleted chat records and changed usernames.
Police advised the public to verify ticket sellers, avoid payments to unknown individuals and contact the National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) on 997 if they suspect fraud.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rosmah lodges police report over US$13mil luxury mansion purchase claims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has lodged a police report over social media allegations linking her to the purported purchase of a US$13 million luxury property for her son-in-law.
The posts, said to have been circulated on X and Facebook, claimed the wife of jailed former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak was involved in financing the acquisition of the property.
Rosmah denied all the allegations, describing them as baseless and unsupported by credible evidence.
"The allegations purporting to implicate me in the use of shell companies, offshore financial structures, or associations with any of the individuals or entities named in these videos are wholly without foundation, utterly false, and maliciously fabricated.
"They are unsupported by a single shred of credible evidence and represent nothing more than a calculated and deliberate campaign to defame me and to bring dishonour upon my family," she said in a statement.
Rosmah said she recalls no involvement in the purchase, financing, transfer or ownership of any such property, and maintained that the allegations were intended to damage her reputation and that of her family.
She said a police report has been lodged and she anticipates further investigations from authorities into the individuals or parties related to the social media post and that she will give full cooperation to ensure a complete and thorough investigation.
She also called for social media platforms to remove, disable and prevent further circulation of the posts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM10mil allocated for programme to support 1,300 retiring armed forces personnel
Article:
SEGAMAT: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) has allocated RM10 million this year for the implementation of its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme, benefiting about 1,300 Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) personnel approaching retirement.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the allocation for the intervention programme forms part of the ministry's efforts to safeguard the welfare and livelihood of military veterans.
He said the WBL programme enables the experience and expertise acquired by personnel throughout their service in the MAF to be formally assessed and recognised in collaboration with higher education institutions.
Under the programme, personnel can be awarded diplomas aligned with their areas of expertise, including culinary arts, hospitality, sports coaching and management, without having to sit for formal examinations.
"Most personnel join the military with only the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification. When they retire at the age of 41 or 42, they enter the job market with the same qualifications, making it difficult for them to compete for employment opportunities.
"Through the WBL programme, they leave service with at least a diploma, with the RM6,500 assessment cost for each individual fully borne by the government," he told a press conference after a working visit to the 4th Royal Artillery Regiment at Segamat Camp here today.
Meanwhile, Mohamed Khaled said the ministry has also revamped the transition training programme conducted by the Armed Forces Veterans Affairs Corporation (Perhebat) by aligning it more closely with industry requirements.
He said the move is intended to ensure that personnel completing the six-month transition training programme can be directly absorbed into suitable employment sectors without having to search for jobs on their own, while earning a dignified income.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zaid Ibrahim vows to reshape Pas image, says party can lead fairer Malaysia
Article:
KOTA BARU: Former de facto law minister Datuk Zaid Ibrahim has pledged to help reshape public perception of Pas, describing the party as capable of governing a multicultural Malaysia based on justice and equality.
Zaid, who officially joined Pas together with 13 other political figures ahead of the Negri Sembilan state election, said he was warmly received by the party's top leadership when he was admitted as a member last night.
"They're warm, sincere and friendly. I will repay their faith. I will work hard to dispel the image of Pas as an extreme anti-democratic party, not suitable for a multicultural Malaysia," he said.
The former Kota Baru member of parliament said Pas was, in his view, the only Malay-majority political party with the determination to address inequality, hegemony and preferential treatment based on class.
"The essence of Islam will be the governing principle," he said.
Zaid added that a Pas-led administration would uphold fairness and reject double standards.
"You will not have, under Pas rule, (a situation) where we are described as equal, but some are more equal than others," he said.
He also expressed confidence that Pas would play a leading role in forming the next federal government.
"Pas will govern Malaysia together with like-minded progressive MPs after the next general election," he said.
Zaid's entry into Pas comes amid a wider political realignment within the opposition bloc following the party's recent decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu and pursue broader alliances ahead of upcoming state polls and the next general election.
His membership was announced alongside
13 other politicians
during a Pas event held in conjunction with preparations for the Negri Sembilan election in Seremban.
Among the others whose memberships were announced were former Negri Sembilan state executive councillor and Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Dr Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek and former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Rahimi Osman.
Also joining Pas are former Perak state executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman, and former Negeri Sembilan Amanah chairman Dr Rosli Yaakup, who is also a former Pas commissioner for the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Onn Hafiz has attended Transport Ministry functions many times before
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit back at Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's claims about not being invited to Transport Ministry events.
The transport minister said he and his party harboured no ill will towards Onn Hafiz despite recent exchanges between the two.
He said DAP would not force anyone to work with the party.
"If he does not want to sit with me, that's okay. DAP does not regard anyone as an enemy. DAP is a pragmatic party and not an arrogant party. We do not force anyone," he said in a video posted on his Facebook page.
The remarks came amid a public exchange after Onn Hafiz said he would rather not hold the menteri besar's post than sit at the same table with DAP in the Johor government.
In a separate Facebook post, Loke also responded to Onn Hafiz's claim that the Johor administration had not been invited to several official Transport Ministry events over the past three years.
Thanking Onn Hafiz for accepting his invitation to ride on the KTM Komuter Selatan service, he said he wished to "refresh" the Johor Umno chief's memory regarding a number of Transport Ministry programmes attended either by the menteri besar himself or representatives of the Johor government.
Loke said Onn Hafiz had attended several major transport-related events, including the Jan 11, 2024 RTS Link ceremony witnessed by the prime ministers of Malaysia and Singapore; the June 30, 2025 event marking the arrival of the first RTS Link train in Singapore; the Aug 23, 2025 launch of the ETS3 service and opening of the KTM Kluang station by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong; and, the Dec 11, 2025 ceremony marking the completion of the Gemas-Johor Baru double-tracking project and the launch of ETS services to Johor Bahru.
He also noted that invitations had been extended for other ministry programmes, including the MyLesen B2 programme for school students in Johor Baru on May 11 this year, but no representative from the state government was present.
Loke said representatives of the Johor government had also attended several Transport Ministry-related events, including the signing of a memorandum of understanding for the Kluang Heritage Railway Linear Park project on Sept 7, 2023, a working visit to the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex Customs, Immigration and Quarantine complex on Jan 11, 2024, and the reopening of the Pasir Gudang-Kempas Baru railway line on May 9, 2025.
"I understand election season can be busy. Perhaps some things were forgotten," he said.
Loke also drew a distinction between official functions and working visits, saying he did not wish to burden the menteri besar with invitations to every working visit undertaken by the ministry.
Nevertheless, he said an official invitation to the launch of the KTM Komuter Selatan service would be delivered promptly and that Onn Hafiz would be invited to deliver a speech at the event in Kulai on Tuesday.
Loke also noted that Johor's executive councillor in charge of the transport portfolio had participated in a trial run of the Komuter Selatan service on March 3 this year, before the service details had been finalised, adding that the ministry had not been informed of the visit.
On Friday, Loke invited Onn Hafiz to join him on a train ride to mark the launch of the KTM Komuter Selatan service from Kulai to Johor Baru.
In response, Onn Hafiz said he was willing to attend and appreciated the invitation, but claimed the state government was invited late, despite the event having been planned more than a week earlier.
The Johor Barisan Nasional chairman also listed several ministry events between 2023 and 2025 which, he claimed, neither he nor the relevant state executive councillor had been invited to attend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tight housing market boosts New York office conversions
Article:
NEW YORK: New York real estate firms are increasingly converting office buildings into apartment complexes as an antidote to the city's housing crunch, tapping into tax incentives also designed to increase the supply of affordable homes.
Water Street in lower Manhattan has been a popular area for the makeovers, earning it the nickname "Conversion Alley", with buildings offering a mix of luxury and affordably-priced apartments to qualify for municipal abatements.
"My belief is just about any building could be converted as residential," Joey Chilelli, partner at real estate developer Vanbarton Group, told AFP during a tour of an office high-rise at 77 Water Street the firm is converting into a residential building.
The real estate retooling has reached celebrated structures such as the Flatiron Building near Madison Square Park, which is currently being converted. Real estate firms view the projects as less risky and quicker to implement than many new builds.
The movement comes as rental costs continue to drift higher. In April, the median rent of a Manhattan apartment topped $5,000 for the first time as the vacancy rate slipped to a six-year low of 1.55 per cent.
By contrast, commercial real estate vacancies stood at 14.6 per cent in the first quarter, reflecting a lingering impact from the pandemic period when remote working depleted downtown business districts.
Under the current policy, the city abates 90 per cent of property taxes on conversions that set aside at least 25 per cent of units as affordable housing. The affordable apartments may rent for around one-quarter of market rates but otherwise look identical.
Such abatement policies have drawn interest from developers, along with a measure that expands the pool of qualifying buildings to those built before 1990 instead of 1961.
- Amenities -
In Manhattan, more than 44 commercial buildings have been converted into residential ones between 2020 and 2024, resulting in nearly 18,000 new apartments.
Real estate firm Cushman & Wakefield expects 31 conversions in 2026 compared with 18 in 2025 and nine in 2024.
In December 2022, former mayor Eric Adams set a target of adding 500,000 new apartments through 2030, while his successor Zohran Mamdani in April unveiled plans for another 200,000 new affordable homes.
Vanbarton began converting buildings in 2013 and currently manages more than 5,000 apartments.
Construction at 77 Water Street is in full swing, with the first residential occupants arriving in October and completion slated for mid-2027.
The 30-floor high-rise will contain 650 apartments, some with a view of the Statue of Liberty.
Chilelli said the firm is anticipating hundreds of thousands of applications for suites designated for affordable housing; the winners will be determined by lottery.
At Pearl House, a 1972 building at 160 Water Street, the conversion has resulted in nearly 600 new apartments since December 2023.
"We are very, very happy here," said renter Charles Wisell.
Wisell, an attorney, originally rented a one-bedroom apartment but has gone for a bigger space to accommodate his wife and son, who now prefer their new abode to their home outside the city.
Wisell raved about the building's amenities, which include a bowling alley, a sports suite featuring simulated golf, a spa with a jacuzzi, a "paw salon" for pets and a "game den" with backgammon, board games and a pool table.
"The location is terrific; you're right in the middle of everything," Wisell said. "I'm walking distance to my office."
- Some affordable units -
At 77 Water, Vanbarton has implemented suggestions from renters, including a room with light therapy in the spa.
At other converted Manhattan buildings, developers have installed basketball courts, Pilates studios and both indoor and outdoor pools.
Such luxury services may be included in the rent or are available to residents for a monthly fee of $100-150.
Monthly rents at Pearl House start at $3,995 for a studio and $6,475 for a one-bedroom. Rents for the affordable units begin at $932 for a studio to $3,286 for a three-bedroom.
In Manhattan, the average price in April for a one-bedroom was $5,228.
The biggest conversion so far is the SoMA, which was built in 1969 and once occupied by JPMorgan Chase and the New York Daily News. It has 1,320 apartments.
But that will be overtaken in 2027 by the former Pfizer building in midtown, which will have around 1,600 apartments.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two decades later, infamous Highland Towers to be demolished by year's end
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The abandoned Highland Towers Condominium in Hulu Kelang will be demolished by the end of the year after the magistrate court approved an application filed by the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ) last week.
Council deputy president Hasrolnizam Shaari said the court's decision paves the way for the long-abandoned structure to be torn down after more than two decades.
He said the council is now in the process of coordinating the next steps especially to the parties involved in the demolition work.
"We will carry out a coordination meeting involving unit owners, represented by the developers and insolvency authorities.
"We don't expect the process to take long as the objective is clear — the demolition will be carried out within this year," he said after the Car-Free Programme held in conjunction with MPAJ's World Environment Day 2026 here today.
Also present was senior political adviser to the prime minister, Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz.
On Dec 11, 1993, the Tower 1 block of Highland Towers came toppled over, killing 48 people. Rescuers, however, managed to find two people alive.
It was later found that soil erosion caused by heavy rains had led to the collapse.
Meanwhile, in his speech, Hasrolnizam reaffirmed the council's commitment to expanding green initiatives that benefit the community.
He said the council carries out various activities, including recycling campaigns, community clean-ups, tree-planting activities and environmental education initiatives that support the sustainable development agenda within its administrative area.
"Today's Car-Free day is not merely a recreational or a leisure event.
"Instead, this reflects the council's commitment to promoting a healthy lifestyle, reducing dependence on motor vehicles and raising public awareness on the importance of environmental conservation," he said.
He said the World Environment Day celebration also allows the public an opportunity to reevaluate their roles and responsibilities in protecting the environment.
"Environmental sustainability is not only the responsibility of the government or local authorities but a shared responsibility of all members of the community.
"Every small action, such as recycling, reducing use of plastic, keeping the surroundings clean and planting trees makes a significant impact on environmental wellbeing," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tropicana strengthens ESG governance with MACC anti-corruption initiative
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tropicana Corp Bhd is reinforcing its commitment to environmental, social and governance (ESG) excellence through a strategic collaboration with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) aimed at strengthening integrity and governance practices across the organisation.
The property developer recently hosted a MACC-led anti-corruption workshop on integrity or ethical conduct and best governance practices, covering key corruption-prevention topics.
"The initiative reflects its commitment to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UNSDG), particularly Goals 4 and 16, which emphasise education, skills development, and strong governance, ethics, and compliance," the company said in the statement.
Tropicana head of ESG unit Serena Lim said the company remains committed to continuously strengthening its ESG and governance practices in line with evolving practices and regulatory requirements.
Lim said to reinforce its governance framework, the company has updated its anti-corruption and bribery policy in line with the latest Malaysian regulations.
"Together with our ESG, human resources, and whistleblowing policies, these are accessible via the Tropicana Employee Portal to ensure transparency and accountability across the organisation," she said.
Held at Tropicana Golf & Country Resort on June 10, 2026, the workshop was attended by 23 employees from relevant departments.
It covered key corruption-prevention topics, including integrity in the workplace, conflict-of-interest awareness, fraud prevention, and corporate liability under the applicable regulatory framework, while also incorporating real-life case studies to enhance practical understanding and awareness.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man detained for buying fuel twice within minutes at Tumpat petrol station
Article:
KOTA BARU: A 43-year-old man was arrested by enforcement officers after allegedly buying subsidised petrol twice, within minutes of each other, at a petrol station in Tumpat this morning.
Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director Hanif Yusabra Yusuf said the suspect was detained after investigations showed him buying 120 litres of petrol at the same petrol station.
"The first purchase was made at 7.42am involving about 80 litres of petrol, followed by a second purchase of about 40 litres at 7.52am. The driver was detained to facilitate investigations," he said.
Hanif said early investigations found that the petrol station had allegedly breached Control of Supplies Regulations 1974 by carrying out repeated sales to the same customer.
He said the petrol station operator is being investigated under Regulation 12A of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974.
"The suspect is being investigated under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961 for possessing controlled goods with the intention of committing an offence," he added.
During the operation, he said 120 litres of petrol worth RM446.40 and the car valued at RM5,000 were seized.
Hanif added records showed that both the driver and the petrol station had previously committed similar offences.
He said the ministry would continue to monitor petrol stations and take stern action against any party found abusing subsidised or controlled goods for unlawful purposes.
"Enforcement operations will be intensified, particularly in border districts, to prevent leakages and ensure government subsidies reach their intended beneficiaries," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian economy comes up for air but struggles to win back investors
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's economy faces a perfect storm wrought by high energy prices, and while the currency has rebounded slightly, critics warn government policies are unnerving investors at a critical moment.
Southeast Asia's biggest economy, a net oil importer, was hit hard by the global surge in crude prices fuelled by the Middle East war.
To shield its citizens, the government has insisted on maintaining a costly subsidy on fuel and a multi-billion-dollar school meal programme criticised for being wasteful as well as causing mass food poisoning.
At a time when Indonesia desperately needs foreign currency, authorities spooked investors with tighter export controls lambasted as "resource nationalism", while a move by parliament to tighten oversight of the central bank raised fears for its independence.
The rupiah has plummeted, hitting successive record lows and dropping below 18,100 to the dollar this week.
The stock market has lost about a third of its value since the start of the year – one of the worst performances globally – as traders increasingly "sell Indonesia".
This week, there was finally some reprieve as the currency and markets reacted positively to the central bank raising its base lending rate by 75 points in back-to-back hikes.
However, "investor concerns over recent domestic policy moves will persist", said an analysis by BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions.
It pointed out the rupiah was still about seven per cent weaker than at the start of the war in February.
"Given that investor concern over domestic policy has not been resolved, we expect depreciatory pressure on the rupiah to persist," BMI said.
This, in turn, would force Bank Indonesia "to hike rates further", it added.
- 'Populist and interventionist' -
High lending rates tend to dampen economic growth.
President Prabowo Subianto's government is chasing a growth target of eight per cent by 2029 as one of its major policy objectives – an ambitious goal some experts warn would be hard to reach.
Deputy Finance Minister Juda Agung told AFP this week the government would not abandon its target despite the high social spending that underpins it.
"We have to grow higher to become a rich country by 2045," he said in an interview. "Otherwise we are... going to be trapped in the middle-income countries" group.
Juda, a former central bank deputy governor, said the government supported the recent interest rate hikes despite the potential risk to growth as well as higher debt servicing costs for the state.
"This is the area of the central bank. They are independent. They know what they should do," he said.
Interest rate hikes alone may not be enough to prop up the rupiah, experts say.
"Placing the currency on a firmer footing requires... the Prabowo administration to shift away from its populist and interventionist policy agenda," Capital Economics said in a note this week.
"Ultimately what's needed is a clear shift... towards more investor-friendly policymaking."
- Rebuilding trust -
Juda insisted the rupiah was undervalued and that economic pressures were "manageable" and will abate once the war ends.
"Our economy is quite resilient," he said.
Economists expect further interest rate hikes, spelling potential trouble for the government's budget deficit, which it is required by law to keep at no more than three per cent of GDP.
Adding to the uncertainty, Jakarta is awaiting a decision by global index compiler MSCI on its market risk status after the group expressed concerns about the transparency of stock ownership.
A downgrade could trigger more capital flight.
According to Juda, there have been "big inflows on the government bond" since the interest rate rose, as well as "signs of confidence in the stock market".
The World Bank said Thursday Indonesia would likely see growth of no more than 5.0 per cent under the strains of high public spending – lower than the government's target of 5.4 per cent.
In the first quarter of 2026, official data put growth at 5.6 per cent, but analysts have expressed doubts about the reliability of the figures.
Deni Friawan, a researcher at the Jakarta-based Centre for Strategic and International Studies, said the government should cut spending to show investors it is committed to keeping the fiscal deficit in check.
"Trust is earned by performance, by reputation, by action, not just with words," he told AFP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Just poverty alleviation must follow need, not race or state lines
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for a deeper understanding of what justice and compassion truly means in the context of poverty alleviation.
Speaking to reporters after officiating the Koperasi Profesional Putrajaya Bhd (Koprojaya) 10th annual general meeting, he said the political discourse in the country had too often reduced the question of justice to one of race or geography.
He said it should instead be guided by where the need was greatest.
"If the majority of the poor are from a particular community, then that is where we must give attention," he said.
Anwar said, while the majority of those living in poverty in Malaysia were still Malay Bumiputera, significant pockets of poverty also existed within the Indian community and among the Orang Asli.
"These must be addressed. That is why I believe the understanding of what justice and compassion truly mean is very critical," he said.
On governance within the cooperative sector, Anwar said the performance of cooperatives was directly tied to how well they were managed, adding that corruption and abuse of power remained critical concerns for him.
He acknowledged that disagreements on the matter existed both within and outside the government, but warned that without correcting these failings, even large amounts of money generated would fail to benefit the wider public.
"It will only flow to the same small group. That is why I say governance is important," he said.
He said this after congratulating Koprojaya, saying it was among the best-governed cooperatives in the country.
"It is among the cooperatives that is led and managed in a very orderly manner and with good governance," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UAE AI push opens Gulf opportunities for Malaysian firms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The United Arab Emirates (UAE), one of the Middle East's leading artificial intelligence (AI) investment hubs, is channelling billions of dollars into digital infrastructure, sovereign cloud systems and smart city initiatives as it accelerates efforts to diversify its economy and strengthen long-term digital competitiveness.
The rapid expansion of the UAE's digital ecosystem is expected to create opportunities for Malaysian companies to participate in AI-ready data centre projects, smart infrastructure and digital sovereignty initiatives, particularly as Gulf governments ramp up spending on advanced technologies.
Market observers said Malaysia's strengths in semiconductor manufacturing, electrical and electronics (E&E), digital services and smart infrastructure could position local firms as engineering partners, systems integrators and technology providers supporting the region's digital transformation agenda.
The recently concluded UAE Data Centre Infrastructure & Cloud Summit 2026 is also expected to open further avenues for Malaysian firms seeking expansion in the Gulf, paving the way for potential technology partnerships and joint ventures.
Held in Abu Dhabi on June 11, the summit brought together senior government officials, regulators and technology executives to discuss investments in AI, data infrastructure, cloud systems and smart technologies amid rising regional demand for digital transformation.
The event underscored the Gulf region's growing focus on sovereign digital capabilities, AI-ready infrastructure and cloud ecosystems, as governments move to reduce dependence on oil revenues and build technology-driven economies.
US-based Robo.ai Inc. and its wholly owned data processing and compression subsidiary, Neurovia AI, strengthened their regional positioning ahead of the summit through the appointment of Emirati executive Rashed Aleghfeli as chief operating officer, where he will oversee operational management and strategic execution amid accelerating demand for AI infrastructure.
During the summit, Aleghfeli delivered a presentation titled "Unload Data Burden, Unlock AI Power: Building a Resilient AI Infrastructure in UAE", outlining Neurovia AI's strategy for foundational data platforms for physical AI — systems designed to enable machines, sensors and autonomous technologies to process visual data more efficiently.
"The UAE is building the most ambitious digital infrastructure in the world, and the data layer beneath it must be trusted, efficient, and sovereign. That is precisely what Neurovia AI delivers," he said in a statement.
"I am committed to driving the operational foundations that will bring NeuroStream to governments and enterprises across the region and beyond," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuziah: PKR will contest 20 seats in Johor, 16 in Negri Sembilan
Article:
KUANTAN: PKR will contest at least 20 seats in Johor and 16 seats in the Negri Sembilan elections, said its secretary-general, Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh.
She said all seat negotiations among Pakatan Harapan component parties have been finalised and concluded in good spirits.
"I understand that the latest number of seats PKR will contest in Negri Sembilan is 16, while the minimum in Johor is 20... all sorted out," she told reporters after opening a prominent supermarket chain in Bandar Kuantan Putri here today.
PH had previously announced that it will contest all 56 seats in the Johor election and all 36 seats in Negri Sembilan.
Meanwhile, Fuziah said she expects voter turnout for the Johor election to increase compared with the previous state election, which was held when the country was still affected by Covid-19.
"Voters working in Singapore did not travel to vote in 2022 due to health concerns, and I am confident of a higher voter turnout this time which will favour PH," she said.
On the entry of Parti Bersama Malaysia and Parti Wawasan Negara into the political landscape, Fuziah said Malaysia had long been dominated by a single political coalition before the landscape began to change after the 2008 general election.
"A stronger two-coalition system emerged and provided a credible challenge to Barisan Nasional through the formation of Pakatan Rakyat (later evolved into PH).
"In many mature democracies, a multi-party system is the norm. It is also common for different coalitions to govern at the federal and state levels, and this should not be seen as unusual in Malaysia," she said.
Fuziah said political competition should be conducted in a healthy manner, with parties focusing on ideas and policies that benefit the people rather than attacking or undermining opponents.
Yesterday, former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin announced the formation of Parti Wawasan Negara, which will contest the Johor and Negeri Sembilan elections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kinabatangan and Crocker Range biosphere reserves important for Sabah
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The proclamation of Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve marks a historic moment and a proud achievement for Sabah and Malaysia.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said
the recognition by Unesco
on the second reserve last year proves that the Kinabatangan landscape, covering approximately 413,866ha, possesses outstanding biodiversity value and ecological significance at the international level.
"With this world-class status, the Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve is expected to attract international attention in the fields of research, education, nature-based tourism and global collaboration opportunities," he said.
Hajiji was speaking at the 12th anniversary celebration of the Crocker Range Biosphere Reserve and the declaration ceremony of the Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve at Padang Merdeka here.
He said the Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve focuses on river and wetland ecosystems, which are important habitats for wildlife and essential for maintaining healthy water systems.
"When forests are protected and allowed to recover and regenerate naturally, they continue to store carbon over the long term," he said, adding that the biosphere also plays an important role in strengthening local economies.
As for the Crocker Range Biosphere Reserve, the 350,584ha area covered 10 districts, namely Tuaran, Kota Kinabalu, Penampang, Ranau, Tambunan, Keningau, Tenom, Beaufort, Papar and Membakut.
The recognition of the Crocker Range Biosphere Reserve has contributed to Sabah attaining the Unesco Triple Crown Sites status.
"In the Crocker Range and Kinabatangan, local communities can benefit through sustainable tourism, including homestays and community-based enterprises.
"These long-term livelihood opportunities encourage communities to continue protecting forests and wildlife because the environment becomes a stable source of income rather than a source of short-term gains.
"Therefore, we must ensure that the recognition of these two biosphere reserves brings benefits and economic spillover effects to all segments of society living within both biosphere reserve areas," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police detain man suspected of abusing stepson
Article:
SEREMBAN: Police have arrested a man on suspicion of abusing his 2-year-old stepson here yesterday.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said police received a report on the incident from the child's biological mother about 8.30pm before arresting the 24-year-old suspect in front of Tuanku Ja'afar Hospital (HTJ) about two hours later.
"Preliminary investigations found that the incident occurred inside a car occupied by the victim, his mother, who is in her 20s, and his stepfather at a petrol station along the Seremban-Jelebu road.
"Investigations also revealed that the victim had allegedly been repeatedly assaulted by the man, who works as a delivery rider, resulting in injuries while at the family's home in Jalan Rasah here on previous occasions," he said in a statement today.
He said a physical examination of the victim found reddish bruising on the front and back of the body, believed to have been caused by pinching. The child is currently receiving treatment at HTJ.
Yatim said the couple had only recently married, tying the knot in April this year.
Meanwhile, he said checks found that the man had no prior criminal record and had tested negative in a preliminary urine screening.
He said the suspect has been remanded for seven days until June 20 to facilitate investigations under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IAEA chief: Malaysia on solid footing to pursue nuclear power
Article:
VIENNA: Malaysia is well-positioned to pursue a nuclear power programme, having built a long-standing tradition and expertise in nuclear technology, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) director-general Rafael Mariano Grossi said.
He said Malaysia's experience in operating research reactor has provided the country with a strong foundation to advance its nuclear energy ambition as part of its future power mix.
"We have been working with your country, and there is a strong interest that has been manifested to us by the government to explore more systematically the possibility of acceding to nuclear energy," he told reporters participating in the IAEA Journalists Seminar held here.
Grossi said having a research reactor was "certainly a very good, solid basis" for Malaysia, as it also meant the country already had people familiar with nuclear technology and reactor operations.
"There are many people in Malaysia who know nuclear technology and know what a nuclear reactor is," he added.
Malaysia operates the Triga Puspati Reactor, the country's only nuclear research reactor, which began operations in 1982 and reached first criticality on June 28 of the same year.
Located at the Malaysian Nuclear Agency in Bangi, the reactor has been used for training, research, isotope production and other scientific applications.
Malaysia has been actively exploring the potential of developing nuclear power as part of strategy to diversify its energy mix, secure clean and reliable electricity supply, and strengthen national energy security agenda.
The ambition was also outlined under the 13th Malaysia Plan (2026-2030), which identifies nuclear energy as a viable source of clean electricity in the national energy mix, with implementation targeted to begin from 2031.
The move comes amid growing electricity demand, the need to reduce dependence on fossil fuels, and Malaysia's commitment to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 as well as concerns over the current global energy crisis caused by the West Asian conflict.
According to IAEA, as of January 2026, about 70 newcomer countries in Africa, Latin America and Asia are either pursuing or exploring nuclear energy as part of their national development agenda.
Malaysia is among 28 countries currently in the decision-making phase, where governments are assessing the feasibility of embarking on nuclear power programmes.
Grossi said the IAEA provides assistance to countries embarking on nuclear power programmes, including helping them develop the necessary capacity and establish national nuclear regulatory bodies.
He highlighted the IAEA's Milestones Approach, established since 2007, which enables countries to develop their nuclear programmes in an orderly and systematic manner by drawing on more than 70 years of global experience in commercial nuclear power operations.
The three-phase framework guides newly embarking countries in developing the legal, regulatory and institutional infrastructure necessary for the safe, secure and sustainable deployment of nuclear power in line with IAEA standards.
Grossi added that there is no "one-size-fits-all" approach for countries pursuing nuclear energy, with the IAEA providing independent and impartial advice according to each country's characteristics and needs.
This includes assisting governments in evaluating reactor technologies and vendor proposals to ensure the selected technology are compatible with national grid capacity, supply chain capabilities and long-term development objectives.
"Nuclear is a marriage for life. Nuclear power plants operate for close to one hundred years. So it means that there will be several generations that will be benefiting from it, working around it and so on and so forth.
"So it's a very consequential decision," he said, describing that making the decision to adopt nuclear energy as a long-term national commitment with  lasting strategic significance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sisters and their husbands held over viral video of maid abuse
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Two married couples were arrested yesterday to facilitate investigations after a video showing three domestic helpers being assaulted in Taman Johor near Tampoi went viral on social media.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the couples, aged between 30 and 34, were arrested at a house in the residential area about 7.30pm by Johor Criminal Investigation Department and Johor Bahru Utara police headquarters policemen.
He said preliminary investigations revealed that the couples were living in the same house and that the female suspects were siblings.
According to him, the assault is believed to have involved three domestic helpers and is suspected to have taken place on July 26 last year.
"The video was recorded by one of the victims but only surfaced on social media yesterday. However, the motive behind the incident remains under investigation," he told reporters at the Iskandar Puteri police headquarters here today.
He said the three domestic helpers, believed to be in their 20s, were suspected to have fled and are believed to still be in the country.
Meanwhile, Rahaman said police had seized mobile phones, clothing belonging to the suspects, a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera and two domestic helpers' passports.
He said all four suspects tested negative for drugs and had no prior criminal records. They have been remanded for four days, beginning today.
The case is being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt, Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation, Section 354 of the Penal Code for assault or use of criminal force with intent to outrage modesty, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act, and Section 12(1)(f) of the Passports Act.
Several video clips went viral on social media yesterday, showing a group of individuals assaulting a female domestic helper who was seated on a sofa inside a house.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tuan Ibrahim: More prominent figures expected to join Pas soon
Article:
KOTA BARU: Pas expects more prominent personalities to join the party following the admission of 14 new members, including several former ministers, political leaders and professionals last night.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said those who have expressed their intentions to join up were from various backgrounds.
"Pas has received encouraging interest from people seeking to be part of its political struggle.
"InsyaAllah, there will be more. They come from various backgrounds," he said.
Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan had earlier announced that the party's membership continued to grow, with the latest intake comprising political leaders, professionals and non-governmental organisation activists.
The latest development comes as Pas intensifies preparations for upcoming state elections in Johor and Negri Sembilan.
In Seremban last night, Pas announced it had
accepted 14 prominent figures
, including former ministers, ex-state executive councillors, political leaders, academics and community activists, into the party.
Takiyuddin made the announcement at a gathering held ahead of the Negri Sembilan election.
Among the prominent figures were former Negri Sembilan state executive councillor and Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Dr Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek, former Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law) Datuk Zaid Ibrahim, and former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Rahimi Osman.
Also among them were former Perak state executive councillor and Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman, and former Negri Sembilan Amanah chairman Dr Rosli Yaakup, who is also a former Pas commissioner for the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Efforts to topple Negri government a form of betrayal
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has maintained that efforts to destabilise the Negri Sembilan government amounted to a form of betrayal, even as Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi rejected suggestions that Umno had acted disloyally.
Anwar said his position was based on what he saw as deliberate attempts to undermine a government that had been functioning in conditions of political and economic stability.
"In the situation we have in Negri Sembilan, there was certainly an effort to maintain political and economic stability, but there were elements working to bring it down. To me, that is a form of betrayal," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after officiating Koperasi Profesional Putrajaya Berhad's (Koprojaya) 10th annual general meeting here today.
Yesterday, Zahid, who is also Umno president,
rejected claims that the party had betrayed
its allies in Negri Sembilan, saying there was no question of disloyalty once a government's term had ended.
He said Barisan Nasional's support for the state government remained in place until the end of its term and that the coalition would continue working within the political alliance while seeking to strengthen its position in the legislative assembly.
Anwar, who is also Pakatan Harapan chairman, expressed concern that the country's focus would be consumed by electoral politics at a time when economic challenges demanded full attention.
"Our focus must be on generating the country's economic growth and the people's problems.
"If over the next three or four months we are consumed by the matter of elections, whereas if we can — within two or three months of an economic crisis — concentrate our efforts, that is what concerns me," he said.
Anwar said the key issue was not the elections themselves but the spirit of cooperation underpinning the unity government.
"If there is no betrayal, then fine. But it means we must deepen the understanding of why cooperation and consensus are important, and when there is a necessity for elections at the right time, then by all means proceed," he said.
According to an online portal, Anwar had earlier described the circumstances surrounding the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan legislative assembly as "political theatre", saying the state government had been forced into an election.
He also questioned why Umno could not have waited for the expiry of the Johor and Negri Sembilan governments' terms before pushing for early polls.
On Friday, caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun claimed that Umno's betrayal of the state government had led to the dissolution of the legislative assembly.
He said a letter purportedly written by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin to Pas leaders had revealed discussions between Perikatan Nasional and Umno on efforts to remove him as menteri besar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Operation to capture tiger near Gerik kampung snares 11 elephants instead
Article:
GERIK: A wildlife operation initially triggered by reports of tiger attacks in Kampung Bongor took an unexpected turn, with authorities capturing 11 wild elephants after the animals moved into village areas and damaged crops.
Kampung Bongor Village Development and Security Committee chairman Mohamad Ghazali Salleh said the presence of elephants was first detected on Thursday night when villagers and officers from the Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) were attempting to set up camera traps near a rubber plantation in the village.
He said the camera traps were installed following complaints from villagers that cattle had been attacked by a tiger about a week earlier, with the big cat again spotted by residents last Wednesday.
He said the first three elephants were seen intruding into the rubber plantation and damaging villagers' banana crops, prompting Perhilitan officers to use tranquilliser darts to capture them on the same night.
"Three elephants were captured that night, and up until today, 11 have been successfully captured. We were also informed by Perhilitan that one more elephant has yet to be captured," he said when contacted today.
He said these elephants are believed to have come from the Amanjaya Forest Reserve and are among those that frequently roam near Batu 11, where the memorial monument is located along the East-West Highway.
He said that although elephants had previously entered the village before, this latest intrusion was the largest in terms of numbers.
Ghazali said he hoped the authorities would assist in building an electric elephant fence to prevent future intrusions of elephants and other wild animals into the village, given its proximity to forest areas that serve as elephant habitats.
"Furthermore, our village is located near the forest reserve, and elephants usually come during fruiting season like now," he said.
He added that it was understood that the translocation operation for the captured elephants would begin tomorrow and is expected to take more than a week, with the animals reportedly to be relocated to Terengganu.
He said the presence of both elephants and tigers in the village not only causes concern among residents but also affects tourists, as the village is a popular hiking destination for Bukit Bongor and for viewing the rafflesia flowers found in nearby forests.
Meanwhile, Perak Perhilitan director Mohd Taufik Abdul Rahman said 10 elephants were captured in Kampung Bongor, while another was captured in Kampung Tanjung Rimbun here.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Continuous border enforcement cuts cross-border smuggling, says Kelantan Domestic Trade chief
Article:
TUMPAT: Round-the-clock surveillance at Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complexes, coupled with stringent inspections of modified vehicles, has helped prevent the smuggling of subsidised fuel and other controlled goods into neighbouring countries.
Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director Hanif Yusabra Yusuf said the continuous cooperation among enforcement agencies had contributed to a decline in cross-border smuggling activities involving subsidised goods.
"Smuggling of subsidised goods has decreased compared with previous years due to the commitment shown by all enforcement agencies.
"However, we do not deny that there are still individuals who are willing to take the risk and attempt to smuggle controlled and subsidised items across the border," he said.
Hanif, however, did not provide statistics.
He said the ministry and other enforcement agencies remained vigilant and would continue taking strict action against offenders.
He said enforcement operations were conducted 24 hours a day at strategic locations, including ICQS facilities and border checkpoints, to curb leakages involving government-subsidised goods.
Hanif said syndicates had altered their modus operandi over the years, including modifying vehicles with larger fuel storage tanks to transport greater quantities of petrol.
"In the past, they commonly used Proton Wira cars, but now some of these vehicles have been modified with oversized tanks capable of carrying significantly more fuel.
"Despite these tactics, enforcement agencies are constantly monitoring the situation and carrying out thorough inspections to detect suspicious vehicles," he said.
Hanif added that the ministry would continue strengthening cooperation with relevant agencies to ensure government subsidies reached their intended recipients and were not exploited by smugglers.
He was asked to comment on Malaysian fuel and other subsidised items continue to be targeted by smugglers particularly in border areas where price differences create opportunities for illegal profits.
Malaysian subsidised petrol was still being sold openly in southern Thailand along the border from Sungai Golok sub-district to Narathiwat province found that
Bottles and small plastic containers filled with petrol were seen displayed on makeshift roadside stalls and wooden racks, indicating that cross-border leakage of subsidised fuel remains a persistent issue despite intensified enforcement efforts.
The price gap between fuel in Malaysia and Thailand continues to be a major pull factor for smugglers.
Subsidised RON95 petrol in Malaysia is sold at RM2.05 per litre, while retail petrol prices in Thailand are currently about 44 baht (about RM5.70) per litre, making Malaysian fuel significantly cheaper and highly attractive for illegal cross-border trade.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Between Trump and a hard place: Fed chair Warsh to lead first rate meeting
Article:
US Federal Reserve chief Kevin Warsh will chair his first meeting of the central bank's rate-setting committee next week caught between a rock and a hard place.
Inflation is at a three-year high but Warsh still faces unrelenting pressure from the White House to lower interest rates.
The bank's 12-member Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) will begin a two-day meeting on Tuesday and is widely expected to hold rates steady as the effects of US President Donald Trump's war on Iran course through the world's largest economy.
Warsh, who was picked by Trump, was sworn in last month and has an ambitious and wide-ranging reform agenda.
He has previously expressed support for lowering rates -- in line with Trump's demands -- but will likely face resistance from a divided committee.
At the FOMC's last meeting in April, the Fed held rates steady at 3.50 to 3.75 percent, but the decision saw four voices of dissent -- the largest number since 1992.
Analysts expect the FOMC to deliver a similar decision in June, though with debate expected on whether to change the Fed's guidance on what its next move could be -- a rate hike or a cut.
"He was appointed as Trump's pick, because Trump probably was influencing him to cut rates," Dan North, senior economist at Allianz Trade, told AFP.
"I don't see him being able to do that now, especially with inflation data and job growth data, and what the people on the FOMC said last time around with their dissents."
'Family fight'
The Fed has a dual mandate to keep inflation to its long-term two-percent target while ensuring maximum employment.
It typically achieves these goals through interest-rate decisions -- cutting borrowing costs to spur economic activity or raising them to cool prices.
Before the US-Israel war on Iran sent energy prices skyrocketing, markets had priced in at least one rate cut by the end of 2026.
With inflation flaring due to the war, however, the next move is now forecast to be a rate hike by December, according to CME's FedWatch tool.
That will be sure to anger Trump, who has launched an unprecedented assault on the Fed's independence with a criminal probe against Warsh's predecessor and attempting to fire another Fed governor.
Last week, responding to strong US job growth figures that suggested the Fed should focus on inflation, Trump said he still wanted lower rates but would let Warsh "make that decision."
The FOMC decides by majority vote, and even if Warsh argues for a cut, he must convince at least six other policymakers to join him.
At his confirmation hearing, Warsh said he favored "messier meetings," where policymakers could have "a good family fight."
"He's stepping into an environment that's already messy," warned North of Allianz Trade. "I don't think it's the family fight he was talking about."
Nothing can wish it away
Greg Daco, chief economist at EY-Parthenon, told AFP Warsh was unlikely to try to make wholesale changes at the meeting -- his first chance to sit with the entire committee and "share his perspective on the economic landscape."
Warsh has proposed reducing the amount of information the Fed communicates about its decisions: cutting out forward guidance and projections.
"In this first meeting, my guess is that he will withhold his projection, but not necessarily change the way projections are published," said Daco.
While most analysts expect rates to be held steady at this meeting, opinions vary wildly on what the Fed's next move could be -- whether war-fuelled inflation will need to be addressed, or if it can be treated as temporary.
"Delaying rate hikes is riskier today than it was as the economy emerged from the pandemic," warned Diane Swonk, chief economist at KPMG.
"The persistence of inflation is the hand that Warsh has been dealt; nothing can wish it away."
As for whether Warsh will succumb to Trump's pressure, "that's something that's going to have to be tested," said EY-Parthenon's Daco.
"I don't think we know, to be honest, at this stage."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday, Hormuz to open after
Article:
US President Donald Trump said that a deal with Iran to end the war in the Middle East could be signed Sunday, and that the strategic Strait of Hormuz would be "open to all" immediately after.
Iran had offered a different timeline earlier in the day, but nonetheless signalled an agreement was in the offing, as both the warring parties and their mediators expressed increasing optimism that weeks of halting negotiations were drawing to a close.
The leader of key mediator Pakistan said a deal was closer "than ever before".
The "finalisation" of this agreement is expected "within the next 24 hours", Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said Saturday on X, adding that it will be signed electronically, without going into further detail or specifying what this would involve.
He said "technical level talks" are expected to follow next week.
A Pakistani foreign ministry statement also said the signing was planned for Sunday.
The momentum came in spite of fresh skirmishes in the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has blockaded since early in the war, throwing global markets into turmoil.
"The Deal is scheduled to get signed tomorrow, and immediately after it is signed, the Hormuz Strait is OPEN TO ALL," a post on Trump's official Truth Social platform read on Saturday.
Since an April 8 truce paused the worst of the fighting, Trump has repeatedly insisted a deal was imminent, only for the wrangling to drag on.
Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei had said earlier on Saturday that the date of the signing was yet to be determined, but "it will not be tomorrow".
However, he added: "The possibility of this happening in the coming days cannot be ruled out."
The warring parties have nonetheless released conflicting information about the contents of the deal, as each seeks to show it emerged from the war with the upper hand.
Hormuz drones
Tehran has insisted it will maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime trade route for oil and gas shipments from the Gulf.
Since imposing its blockade, Iran has demanded vessels obtain permission from its armed forces before transiting the waterway, and has established a new body to oversee it and collect tolls.
The US has responded with its own blockade of Iranian ports.
The US military's Central Command said earlier Saturday Iran had "launched multiple one-way attack drones in an attempt to strike commercial ships transiting the Strait".
It added that "US forces have downed all of them in recent hours".
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, in an interview with state television Friday, had said the deal on the table called for the lifting of the US naval blockade.
"The administration of Strait of Hormuz will no longer be the same as before," he added, calling the waterway one of Iran's "main instruments of deterrence".
The US has repeatedly said Iran remaining in control of the strait would be unacceptable, and Trump's post made no mention of tolls or other arrangements.
Nuclear dust
Another key sticking point in the talks has been the fate of Iran's nuclear programme, particularly its stockpile of highly enriched uranium -- believed to have been buried by US strikes last year during a previous short-lived war.
Iran has long insisted its nuclear programme is peaceful and that it has a right to enrichment, but the United States, Israel and other Western governments suspect it of seeking a bomb.
Araghchi on Friday said the only way to deal with Iran's enriched uranium "is to dilute it inside Iran".
Trump, who has justified the war as necessary to prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons, previously said the US would remove and destroy the uranium.
In Saturday's post, he said: "When all is calm, we will go in and get the Nuclear Dust... and downblend and destroy it, whether in Iran, or the United States."
"Hopefully, this process will all work out quickly, easily, and smoothly," he added. "If it doesn't, we have the ultimate alternative, hopefully never to be used again!"
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel -- which launched the war in tandem with the US in February -- said Trump had promised him any agreement would include the removal of the enriched nuclear material.
In the streets of Tehran, there was scepticism the latest agreement would cross the finish line.
"I don't think there is any deal soon," said Saeed Sadeghi, 49. "I don't trust their word."
Fars news agency shared a video from Iran's northeastern city of Mashhad showing dozens protesting the deal outside a foreign ministry building Saturday.
It showed women in black chadors chanting "death to dishonourable Araghchi, the infiltrator", while waving red and black flags.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Activist to make 290km Taiping-Parliament run for 3 elephants in Japan
Article:
IPOH: Animal welfare advocate Neow Choo Seong will undertake a 290km solo run from Taiping to Parliament in Kuala Lumpur in a bid to draw greater attention to the welfare and future of three elephants currently housed in Japan.
Describing Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) as part of Malaysia's natural heritage, Neow said the six-day endurance challenge aims to raise public awareness and galvanise support for efforts to bring the elephants home.
Organised by the Solidariti Bersama DAK Secretariat, the run will see Neow cover about 290km from Perak to Selangor before arriving at Parliament on June 22, the opening day of the next Dewan Rakyat sitting.
Neow, who is also MCA Youth information chief, said the initiative seeks to highlight growing concerns over the welfare and well-being of the elephants, which were loaned to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka.
He said the campaign was intended to urge the government and relevant authorities to play a more active role in securing the future of Dara, Amoi and Kelat.
Neow said the run was not merely a test of physical endurance but a symbolic journey to remind Malaysians of their responsibility towards the country's wildlife heritage.
"This run is not about endurance alone. It is a journey of solidarity and a plea for compassion.
"Dara, Amoi and Kelat are part of Malaysia's natural heritage. Since their transfer to Japan on March 11, there have been increasing concerns regarding their health and well-being.
"We believe the matter deserves urgent attention and should be openly tabled for debate in Parliament," he said in a statement today.
Neow expressed hope that Malaysians from all walks of life would unite behind the cause and help amplify calls for the elephants' welfare to be safeguarded, while every possible effort is made to bring them home.
The run will begin in Taiping on June 17 and proceed through Ipoh, Kampar, Tapah, Tanjung Malim and Selayang before concluding at Parliament in Kuala Lumpur.
Beyond the physical challenge, the initiative will serve as a travelling awareness campaign, with Neow engaging local communities, supporters and members of the public at various stops along the route.
He said the programme aims to encourage broader discussions on wildlife conservation and reinforce the importance of protecting species that form part of Malaysia's natural legacy.
"The campaign will culminate with the presentation of a public petition at the main entrance of Parliament," he said.
The petition will be handed over to Ayer Hitam member of parliament Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong, who is also MCA president, in the hopes that the concerns raised by the public can be brought before the Dewan Rakyat for deliberation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three factory buses destroyed in Serendah industrial area fire
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three factory buses were destroyed in a fire at an industrial area in Taman Melati Desa Kiambang in Serendah early today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said firefighters received a distress call at 2.42am, adding that personnel were despatched from the Kuala Kubu Baru and Bukit Sentosa stations to the scene.
"The fire involved three factory buses parked within a factory compound.
"One of the buses was 90 per cent burnt at the time firemen arrived, while the other two buses were almost completely destroyed," he said, adding that no casualties were reported.
[[nid:1462989]]
He said firemen, led by Mohd Yusof Md Ali, took about 15 minutes to bring the fire under control.
"Ten firefighters, assisted by a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) from Kuala Kubu Baru and a Compact Fire Rescue Tender (CFRT) from Bukit Sentosa, were involved in the operation," he said.
Mukhlis said overhaul works were still being carried out at the time of reporting, while the cause of the fire and estimated losses are under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Firefighters race to locate four women lost on Bukit Tunggul
Article:
SEPANG: Firefighters are searching for four women who became lost while hiking at Bukit Tunggul in Lorong Bahagia, Jenderam Hilir, near here today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the hikers had lost their way while descending the hill.
He said a team from the Dengkil Fire and Rescue Station was deployed to the scene and arrived at 10.50am.
"The victims, aged between 25 and 40, have been successfully contacted and are believed to be safe.
"However, they remain unable to find their way out of the hiking trail.
"Our personnel are currently conducting search efforts to locate and guide them to safety," he said when contacted.
Mukhlis said six firefighters, led by operations commander Muhamad Sakirin Selamat, were involved in the operation.
The search operation was still ongoing at the time of writing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Blood, sweat and tears — and lost cash: KL police tracing mule accounts in BTS ticket scam probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in connection with alleged fraud involving the sale of tickets for BTS’ upcoming concert in Malaysia.
According to
Berita Harian
, Brickfields district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing and several bank accounts suspected of being involved had already been identified.
“We suspect these accounts were used by the syndicate to receive payments from victims.
“It is possible that the owners of these bank accounts are mule account holders,” he said to the national daily today.
Hoo said no new police reports had been lodged in relation to the case so far.
South Korean boy band BTS are scheduled to perform in Kuala Lumpur on December 12 and 13 as part of their BTS World Tour ‘Arirang’, with the concerts set to take place at TM Stadium Nasional in Bukit Jalil.
Earlier, police advised the public to remain cautious and avoid purchasing BTS concert tickets from unofficial sources or through so-called agents to prevent falling victim to scams.
Hoo had previously said that, as of June 8, police had received 28 reports from individuals claiming they had been cheated in transactions involving tickets for the concerts.
Media reports had earlier highlighted how tickets for the highly anticipated shows sold out within a short period, creating opportunities for scammers to target fans eager to secure seats for the December performances.
Tickets for the concerts were sold through the organiser’s website at prices ranging from RM338 to RM1,288.

Title: Police identify two bodies found in Kedah River, rule out link between deaths
Article:
ALOR SETAR, June 14 — Police have identified one of two men whose bodies were recovered from the Kedah River today as a 16-year-old boy who had been reported missing two days earlier, adding that there was no indication the deaths were linked.
Harian Metro
reported Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali as saying that the teenager, identified as Saw Chee Wan, was believed to have left his home on June 12. Family members identified him through a pendant necklace he had been wearing.
According to police, Saw had a learning disability and was reported missing from his home in Kampung Seberang Perak, about two kilometres from where his body was found.
The teenager's body was discovered by Alor Setar City Council cleaning workers at about 9am near Taman Pesisiran Jeragan in Ampang Jajar.
Firefighters later recovered the body from the river.
The national daily also said that police later identified the second victim as Muhammad Amin, also known as N. Thiagarah, 46, from Tanjung Bendahara who had also been reported missing on June 12.
Syed Basri said investigations found that Muhammad Amin had previously received treatment for mental health issues.
Although the two bodies were found about 100 metres apart, police said investigations showed the cases were unrelated.
A forensic examination found no evidence of foul play, and both cases have been classified as sudden deaths.
The bodies were taken to Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital for post-mortem examinations.
Police also confirmed that a motorcycle found near the scene belonged to Muhammad Amin, based on identification by his younger brother.
Syed Basri urged the public not to speculate about the circumstances surrounding the discovery of the two bodies.
Earlier reports said the bodies of two men, aged 16 and 46, were found in the Kedah River near Titi Taman Pesisiran Jeragan today.

Title: Court ruling clears way for Highland Towers demolition before year end, says MPAJ
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The remaining blocks of the Highland Towers condominium in Hulu Kelang are expected to be demolished before the end of the year, following a Magistrates’ Court decision allowing the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ) to proceed with the long-delayed plan.
MPAJ deputy president Hasrolnizam Shaari said the court approved the council’s application last week, paving the way for the demolition of the abandoned structures that have stood vacant for more than three decades.
“We will organise a coordination meeting involving the unit owners, represented by the developer and the insolvency department.
“We expect this matter will not take long because everyone is clear that this is the objective that has been set. Within this year, the demolition process will take place,” he told
Harian Metro
today.
Hasrolnizam said MPAJ was now coordinating the next steps, particularly with the parties that would manage and carry out the demolition works.
The decision marks the latest development in a case that has remained unresolved since the Highland Towers tragedy in 1993.
One of the condominium’s three blocks collapsed on December 11 that year after a landslide triggered by prolonged heavy rain, killing 48 people.
The two remaining blocks were evacuated following the collapse and have remained abandoned ever since, while efforts to demolish the structures were delayed by legal and administrative issues.
